spec_match.rationale: The find pattern flows through `RegExp.escape` (when regex-mode is off), so a user typing `version(1.0)` matches that exact literal; toggling regex-mode swaps back to raw `new RegExp(input)`. This is the canonical use of the proposal.

interactive.rationale: Real find/replace tool — text inputs for find and replace, three option checkboxes (regex/case/whole-word), nav prev/next buttons, replace-all button, live highlighted document, and a debug strip showing the escaped pattern.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises `Type any literal string ... Toggle Regex mode to disable auto-escaping. Side-by-side shows what a naive new RegExp(input) does` — the page provides exactly that plus a comparison block.

edge_cases.rationale: Side-by-side describes the failure mode in prose but doesn't render it live (the naive panel is text, not a working try-it). Whole-word + regex-mode interaction is also under-explored — `\b` around a user `.` does odd things.

references.rationale: The `see also` list links only sibling concepts. The chromestatus.com/feature/5179154657705984 link required by CLAUDE.md invariant #3 is missing from the page entirely.

design_system.rationale: Uses raw hex / OKLCH colours that bypass the design system: `#fff5f5`, `#ffeef0`, `#991b1b`, `#e6f4ea`, `#065f46`, and `oklch(...)` for the mark highlights. CLAUDE.md invariant #5 mandates only the defined CSS variables. Additionally `.nav-btn` overrides `background` without overriding `color`, which is the exact light-on-light trap invariant #6 calls out.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex / OKLCH with CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: The page uses `#fff5f5`, `#ffeef0`, `#991b1b`, `#e6f4ea`, `#065f46`, and `oklch(...)` for the compare-head and mark highlights. CLAUDE.md invariant #5 only allows the variables defined in `public/styles.css` (`--bg-ivory/paper/stone`, `--text-*`, `--accent-blue/emerald/rose`). Swap each call site to the closest existing accent variable.

openQuestions.title: Fix the .nav-btn background-without-color override
openQuestions.detail: `.nav-btn` sets `background: var(--bg-stone)` but does NOT override the global `button` rule's `color: var(--bg-ivory)`. Result: light-on-light text on the prev/next buttons. CLAUDE.md invariant #6 — when overriding button background, override `color` too. Set `color: var(--text-black)` alongside.

openQuestions.title: Add the missing chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: Invariant #3: every page must cite `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5179154657705984` so the slug-recovery script can recover this folder from its content. Add it to `see also` along with the TC39 proposal.

openQuestions.title: Make the naive-vs-safe comparison a live try-it
openQuestions.detail: The `bad` compare-cell is prose only. Turn it into a small live pane that runs the same Find input through `new RegExp(input)` and shows the (wrong) matches alongside the (right) escaped matches — the rest of the regexp-escape concept folder has this side-by-side pattern and it's far more convincing than text.

openQuestions.title: Cover whole-word + metacharacter interaction
openQuestions.detail: Toggling `Whole word` wraps the escaped (or raw) source in `\b...\b`. For user input like `a.b` in regex-mode + whole-word, the resulting `\ba.b\b` will still match across non-word boundaries. Add an explicit demo of how `RegExp.escape` interacts with `\b` boundaries when the literal starts/ends with non-word characters (e.g. `$price`).

summary: Functionally the most complete regexp-escape demo in the folder — real find, replace, nav, and toggle for regex mode — but it ships three of the design-system invariants flagged in CLAUDE.md: raw hex/OKLCH colours, a button override that strands `color`, and no chromestatus self-heal link. None are content issues, all are mechanical fixes; once cleaned up this is the headline concept page for the feature.
